summary: OCME's Forensic Biology (DNA) Laboratory saw turnaround times worsen starting in FY25 after identifying a case-to-case contamination incident, which it classified as a "significant event" and investigated through a formal Root Cause Analysis completed in November 2024. The agency reports the resulting quality-control holds, combined with a new laboratory information management system installed in July 2025 that required staff retraining, pushed DNA homicide case turnaround from 47 days in FY24 up to 79 in FY25 and further in FY26, even as the lab continued to meet its target for these cases.

sources.label: NYC Office of Chief Medical Examiner, Fiscal 2025 Mayor's Management Report
NYC Office of Chief Medical Examiner, Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Report
